operation: First, create a sketch of an 8.425 radius circle on the XY workplane, then extrude this sketch 3 units upward to form the base flange. Next, select the top Z-axis face of the extruded base, sketch a 6.125 radius concentric circle, and extrude this sketch 18.0 units upward to form the upper cylindrical body. After that, select the top Z-axis face of the upper cylinder, sketch a 4.725 radius concentric circle, and perform a cut-through-all operation to create a full-length central through hole. Finally, select the topmost Z-axis edge and apply a 0.5 unit chamfer.
geometry: This is a fully concentric cylindrical structure made of two stacked coaxial sections: a shorter, wider base cylinder at the bottom, and a taller, narrower upper cylinder mounted on top of the base. A continuous central axial through hole runs the entire vertical length of the part, and the top edge at the intersection of the upper cylinder and the through hole has a small angled chamfer. All features share the same central vertical axis.
function: This is a flanged hollow cylindrical component intended for use as a bushing, spacer, or through-hole fitting. Its wide base provides stable mounting, the central bore accommodates fasteners, wiring, or fluid flow, and the top chamfer eases insertion of mating parts.
